result:
**Goal: Add a Black Hole Accretion Disk & Gravitational Lensing simulation mode.**

This 41k-line single-file Python project (`life.py`) is a terminal-based simulation engine with 65+ modes spanning cellular automata, physics, fluid dynamics, and more. It already has N-Body Gravity and Galaxy Formation, but nothing covering relativistic gravity effects.

The new mode would simulate:
- A central black hole with an event horizon
- Orbiting particles forming an accretion disk (with relativistic precession)
- Gravitational lensing distortion of background stars
- Relativistic jets along the polar axis
- Hawking radiation particles escaping near the horizon

This fits naturally into the Physics & Waves category and would be visually striking in ASCII. Want me to build it?
